tagline: AI standups in Slack that save time and surface blockers.
overview: StandupSpark is a Slack-based AI standup assistant that replaces daily status meetings with quick DM check-ins and clean, human-sounding summaries in your team channel. It respects time zones, nudges gently, highlights blockers, and connects to your tools so managers and teammates can see what matters at a glance.

audience.primary: Engineering and product teams at startups and SMBs working remote or hybrid

problems: Daily standup meetings eat into focus time and multiply across time zones.
Asynchronous updates scatter across Slack threads and are easy to miss.
Blockers get buried; managers waste time chasing status.
Bots feel noisy or rigid; reminders become nagging.

features.name: Slack DM check-ins
features.description: Private, scheduled questions sent via Slack DM; answers post to a shared summary
features.benefit: Team replies in under a minute without breaking flow
features.so_what: You reclaim the 15–30 minutes normally lost to standup and keep momentum

features.name: AI channel summaries
features.description: Clear, human-sounding daily digest with what’s done, what’s next, and blockers
features.benefit: Everyone scans one clean brief instead of reading threads
features.so_what: Leaders spot risks early and teammates see where they can help

features.name: Timezone‑aware scheduling
features.description: Check-ins adapt to each member’s local start time and skip PTO/holidays
features.benefit: No more pings at 2 AM; fewer missed updates
features.so_what: Async teams stay connected without hurting focus or morale

features.name: Smart nudges (low-noise)
features.description: Gentle reminders that respect quiet hours and stop once you reply
features.so_what: You get a complete picture daily without policing the team

features.name: Blocker alerts
features.description: Automatic highlight of blockers with optional manager ping
features.so_what: Issues move sooner; cycle time shortens

features.name: Integrations
features.description: Jira, GitHub, Linear, and Asana context linked in summaries

features.name: Participation analytics
features.description: Simple dashboard for response rates and time saved
features.benefit: See adoption and impact without spreadsheets

features.name: Privacy controls
features.description: Private-by-default answers; data retention controls; skip sensitive channels
features.benefit: People share honest updates without worry

differentiators: Summaries that read like a human wrote them — not a wall of bullets
Zero‑noise mode: post one clean digest instead of individual messages
Gentle, respectful nudges that auto-skip PTO and holidays
Catch‑up brief: one tap to see what you missed while in meetings
Fast, simple setup with opt-in privacy defaults

security.privacy_defaults: Private by default; you choose what posts to a public channel
security.data_use: We process updates to generate summaries and never use your data to train shared models
security.retention: Admin-set retention window (30/60/90 days) with early purge option

free.limits: Up to 10 active users, 1 daily summary, basic analytics

success_metrics: Cut standup meeting time by ~15 minutes per day
Reach 90%+ response rate within two weeks

positioning.why_now: Remote and async teams need time back without losing alignment
